core_limitation: Observed default labels exist only for applicants/accounts that entered the historical booked-loan population. Rejected applicants do not have observed loan outcomes in this dataset.
interpretation_impact: Validation metrics are conditional on the observed accepted/booked population.
Default probabilities should not be interpreted as fully through-the-door population risk.
Thresholds and calibration may shift if rejected applicants are later included.
Fairness and segment conclusions may be incomplete if rejected applicants differ by segment.
portfolio_decision: Do not invent rejected-applicant labels. Document the selection-bias limitation and define production methods that require additional data, policy review, and compliance approval.

production_methods.benefit: Simple to explain and commonly discussed in credit-risk workflows.
production_methods.description: Assign inferred good/bad outcomes to rejected applicants within score bands using observed accepted-applicant bad rates adjusted by policy or bureau evidence.
production_methods.risk: Can reinforce historical policy bias if parceling assumptions are wrong.

production_methods.benefit: Captures uncertainty rather than forcing a single inferred label.
production_methods.description: Add rejected applicants multiple times with fractional outcome weights based on estimated default probability or external performance signals.
production_methods.risk: Requires strong assumptions and careful calibration; can overstate certainty.

production_methods.benefit: Anchors rejected outcomes in observed external behavior.
production_methods.description: Use later bureau performance or external repayment/default signals to observe whether rejected applicants defaulted elsewhere.
production_methods.risk: Requires data contracts, identity matching, observation windows, and consent/legal review.

production_methods.benefit: Produces cleaner labels for policy expansion and bias reduction.
production_methods.description: Approve a controlled, risk-managed sample near the decision boundary to observe future repayment outcomes.
production_methods.risk: Requires risk appetite, ethics review, operational controls, and compliance approval.

production_methods.benefit: Explicitly models the accepted-applicant selection mechanism.
production_methods.description: Model approval/acceptance probability and use inverse-probability or selection-bias corrections when estimating default risk.
production_methods.risk: Requires rejected-applicant features and a reliable acceptance policy history.
